tickerText: [TENNIS] Queen's men's draw heats up: Cam Norrie falls to Davidovich Fokina; Adrian Mannarino upsets No. 3 Jakub Mensik in three tie-break sets; Alex de Minaur and Tommy Paul advance to round of 16 • [BADMINTON] Macau Open Day 2: round-of-32 action across all five disciplines at the East Asian Games Dome — India's Arjun/Amsakarunan topple 7th-seeded Popov brothers in men's doubles • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals LIVE in Paris — knockout format debuts at Centquatre with Asal–ElShorbagy and Orfi–Gilis opening quarterfinals tonight on the all-glass court • [PICKLEBALL] Beijing Open Day 1 underway at China's National Tennis Center: Zane Ford leads men's singles as PPA Tour Asia brings $70K pro prize money to the Olympic venue • [TABLE TENNIS] Kanak Jha competes at WTT Star Contender Ljubljana (June 16–21) at world No. 24; United States Smash tickets on sale for Ontario, CA (June 26–July 5); Shanghai to host 2026 World Table Tennis for Health Festival Oct 20–25

newsItems.title: Norrie Out, Mannarino Stuns Mensik at Queen's
newsItems.excerpt: British No. 1 Cam Norrie's grass-court campaign ended in the first round as fourth seed Alejandro Davidovich Fokina won 7-6 (6), 6-2 on the Andy Murray Arena. Adrian Mannarino produced the upset of the day, knocking out third seed Jakub Mensik 5-7, 7-6 (3), 7-6 (5), while top seed Alex de Minaur and 2024 champion Tommy Paul advanced to the round of 16.

newsItems.title: PSA Squash Tour Finals Go Live in Paris
newsItems.excerpt: The season-ending PSA Squash Tour Finals opened June 17 at Centquatre-Paris — the event's first staging in France and first in Europe since 2013. World champions Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi headline a new straight-knockout, best-of-five format on an all-glass ASB ShowCourt, with $710,000 in combined prize money on the line through June 20.

newsItems.title: Beijing Open Debuts Pickleball at Olympic Venue
newsItems.excerpt: The Capital Securities Beijing Open launched June 17 at China's National Tennis Center — the 2008 Olympic grounds — marking PPA Tour Asia's first event in the capital. U.S. top-10 player Zane Ford tops the men's singles draw as $70,000 in pro prize money and 500 tour points are up for grabs through Championship Sunday on June 21.

newsItems.title: Jha at Ljubljana as US Smash and Shanghai Loom
newsItems.excerpt: World No. 24 Kanak Jha is competing at WTT Star Contender Ljubljana (June 16–21) after his Skopje runner-up finish lifted him six spots in the rankings. The American star is among the homegrown names confirmed for United States Smash at the Ontario Convention Center (June 26–July 5), while the ITTF Foundation announced Shanghai will host the 2026 World Table Tennis for Health Festival on October 20–25.
